content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 18, 1994

Honorable Quentin L. Kopp
Member of the Senate
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA 94814

Re: Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No. A-94-063

Dear Senator Kopp:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform
Act. (Government Code Sections 81000-91015.)

This will confirm that your letter dated February 23, 1994,
accurately summarizes my advice that:

1. The Act does not prohibit you from controlling a ballot
measure committee and does not limit the number of ballot measure
committees you may control; and

2. The Kopp for Senate Committee may make monetary and/or
non-monetary contributions to the BART to the Airport Committee,
including the donation of office space and/or inclusion of
advertising in a Kopp for Senate Committee billboard.

If you have additional questions, please do not hesitate to
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

[signature]
By: Carla Wardlow
Chief, Technical Assistance
Division

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.conclusion: The Act does not prohibit you from controlling a ballot measure committee and does not limit the number of ballot measure committees you may control; and The Kopp for Senate Committee may make monetary and/or non-monetary contributions to the BART to the Airport Committee, including the donation of office space and/or inclusion of advertising in a Kopp for Senate Committee billboard.
sections.question_synthetic: Senator Kopp seeks confirmation of telephone advice regarding whether the Political Reform Act prohibits him from controlling multiple ballot measure committees and whether his campaign committee may make monetary and non-monetary contributions to a ballot measure committee.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirms that the Political Reform Act does not prohibit a legislator from controlling multiple ballot measure committees or limit the number such committees. A campaign committee may make monetary and non-monetary contributions to another committee, including in-kind donations of office space and advertising.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION section headers, but document clearly confirms prior telephone advice with two substantive rulings. Letter format with bracketed file number and proper FPPC letterhead.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Senator Kopp seeks confirmation of telephone advice regarding whether the Political Reform Act prohibits him from controlling multiple ballot measure committees and whether his campaign committee may make monetary and non-monetary contributions to a ballot measure committee.

CONCLUSION: The Act does not prohibit you from controlling a ballot measure committee and does not limit the number of ballot measure committees you may control; and The Kopp for Senate Committee may make monetary and/or non-monetary contributions to the BART to the Airport Committee, including the donation of office space and/or inclusion of advertising in a Kopp for Senate Committee billboard.

Kopp Member of the Senate State Capitol Sacramento, CA 94814 Re: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice Our File No. A-94-063 Dear Senator Kopp: You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided to you concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act. (Government Code Sections 81000-91015.) This will confirm that your letter dated February 23, 1994, accurately summarizes my advice that: 1. The Act does not prohibit you from controlling a ballot measure committee and does not limit the number of ballot measure committees you may control; and 2. The Kopp for Senate Committee may make monetary and/or non-monetary contributions to the BART to the Airport Committee, including the donation of office space and/or inclusion of advertising in a Kopp for Senate Committee billboard. If you have additional questions, please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662. Sincerely, Steven G. Churchwell General Counsel [signature] By: Carla Wardlow Chief, Technical Assistance Division 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
embedding.summary: This is an FPPC confirmation letter to Senator Quentin Kopp confirming prior telephone advice regarding campaign committee contributions and control of ballot measure committees under the Political Reform Act.
